title: Mechanical Engineer
description: A Mechanical Engineer in India works on designing, developing, and testing mechanical systems, machines, and devices. They spend most of their time in offices, laboratories, and manufacturing plants, analyzing data, creating prototypes, and collaborating with teams to solve complex problems. Their work involves a mix of creativity, technical expertise, and problem-solving skills, often under tight deadlines and budget constraints.
degree_name: Bachelor of Engineering in Mechanical Engineering
top_private_colleges: Sri Sivasubramaniya Nadar College of Engineering (SSN)

career_progression.year_0_2: Internships, entry-level positions in manufacturing, design, or research and development.
career_progression.year_3_5: Mid-level positions in design, development, or project management, with opportunities for specialization.
career_progression.year_6_10: Senior-level positions in leadership, management, or technical expertise, with opportunities for entrepreneurship or global roles.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Starting a business in the mechanical engineering space, such as designing and manufacturing custom machinery or developing innovative products.
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities for working in international companies, collaborating with global teams, or pursuing higher education abroad.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Challenging to find internships and entry-level positions, with intense competition and high expectations.
common_misconceptions: Mechanical engineering is only about manufacturing.
Mechanical engineers only work in factories.
